timeline_html:
Summary. Rachel’s shedding began over a year ago and has tended to come in waves. She’s noticed new baby hairs recently, suggesting her scalp is beginning to recover. 🌱

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (resolved; May–Dec 2025)

Timeline forecast. The stress period ran from May to December 2025, with shedding typically peaking in late summer (around August 2025) and calming over the following 3–6 months. Stabilization would be expected by early to mid‑2026, and early regrowth—already visible—should continue improving through mid‑2026. Dominant driver: Chronic stress, because its timing aligns closely with the onset of shedding waves and it lasted several months before easing.

Where you are now. You’re in the early regrowth phase—shedding should already be lighter, and those new baby hairs are a great sign that follicles are re‑activating. Keep nurturing your scalp and stress balance; steadier months ahead will help your hair continue to thicken. 🌱
